title: Finding the Opposite of an Integer

steps.instruction: Define the opposite of a number.
steps.work: The opposite of a number is the number that is the same distance from 0 but on the other side of the number line.
steps.explanation: Every number has exactly one opposite. The opposite of 0 is 0.

steps.instruction: Determine the distance from 0 and flip the direction.
steps.work: -6 is 6 units to the left of 0. Its opposite is 6 units to the right of 0, which is 6.
steps.explanation: To find the opposite, change the sign: negative becomes positive, positive becomes negative.

steps.subgoalLabel: Verify with the additive inverse property
steps.explanation: A number plus its opposite always equals zero. This confirms that 6 is the opposite of -6.

title: Simplifying the Opposite of an Opposite

steps.explanation: The outer negative sign means 'take the opposite of.' The inner part is -4.
Taking the opposite of a negative number gives a positive number.

steps.work: For any number a, -(-a) = a. The opposite of the opposite is the original number.
steps.explanation: Two negatives cancel out. This is like doing an 'undo' — flip once to get the opposite, flip again to return.
